title: Friesland (district)

infobox.value: Sven Ambrosy ([SPD](/wiki/Social_Democratic_Party_of_Germany "Social Democratic Party of Germany"))

is a [district](/wiki/Districts_of_Germany "Districts of Germany") (*Landkreis*) in [Lower Saxony](/wiki/Lower_Saxony "Lower Saxony"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). It is bounded by (from the southeast and clockwise) the districts of [Wesermarsch](/wiki/Wesermarsch "Wesermarsch"), [Ammerland](/wiki/Ammerland "Ammerland"), [Leer](/wiki/Leer_(district) "Leer (district)") and [Wittmund](/wiki/Wittmund_(district) "Wittmund (district)"), and by the [North Sea](/wiki/North_Sea "North Sea"). The city of [Wilhelmshaven](/wiki/Wilhelmshaven "Wilhelmshaven") is enclosed by—but not part of—the district.

["Tabelle EVAS 12411, Fortschreibung des Bevölkerungsstandes auf Basis des Zensus 2022, Stand 31. Dezember 2024"](https://www1.nls.niedersachsen.de/statistik/html/default.asp) (in German). [Landesamt für Statistik Niedersachsen](/wiki/Landesamt_f%C3%BCr_Statistik_Niedersachsen "Landesamt für Statistik Niedersachsen").

District in Lower Saxony, Germany

District in Lower Saxony, Germany

**Friesland** is a [district](/wiki/Districts_of_Germany "Districts of Germany") (*Landkreis*) in [Lower Saxony](/wiki/Lower_Saxony "Lower Saxony"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). It is bounded by (from the southeast and clockwise) the districts of [Wesermarsch](/wiki/Wesermarsch "Wesermarsch"), [Ammerland](/wiki/Ammerland "Ammerland"), [Leer](/wiki/Leer_(district) "Leer (district)") and [Wittmund](/wiki/Wittmund_(district) "Wittmund (district)"), and by the [North Sea](/wiki/North_Sea "North Sea"). The city of [Wilhelmshaven](/wiki/Wilhelmshaven "Wilhelmshaven") is enclosed by—but not part of—the district.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Friesland_(district)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Frisian region was ruled by local chieftains until the 15th century; see [East Frisia](/wiki/East_Frisia "East Frisia") for details.

In 1438 in the northern part of today's Landkreis Friesland the [Lordship of Jever](/wiki/Lordship_of_Jever "Lordship of Jever") was founded. East Frisia was from then on regarded as a hostile territory, and many skirmishes between Jever and East Frisia took place during the 15th and 16th centuries. The last ruler of Jever was Mary of Jever, who ruled until 1575.

After her death Jever became a part of [Oldenburg](/wiki/Duchy_of_Oldenburg "Duchy of Oldenburg"), but East Frisia made a claim for the territory as well. In the following decades East Frisia tried to block all roads between Jever and Oldenburg. It was not before the 17th century that the hostilities between East Frisia and Oldenburg ended.

From 1667 to 1793 Jever was an [exclave](/wiki/Exclave "Exclave") of [Anhalt-Zerbst](/wiki/Anhalt-Zerbst "Anhalt-Zerbst"), and after [Napoleonic occupation](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Holland "Kingdom of Holland") the westernmost exclave of [Prussia](/wiki/Prussia "Prussia"). In 1818 Jever became a part of Oldenburg again.

The southern part of today's District of Friesland is the "Friesische Wehde" ("Frisian Woods"), which also was most of the time part of Oldenburg. This region later formed the District of Varel.

The District was established in 1933 by merging the former districts of Jever and Varel under the name of "Friesland". In 1977 it was dissolved and divided between the neighbouring districts of [Wittmund](/wiki/Wittmund_(district) "Wittmund (district)") and [Ammerland](/wiki/Ammerland "Ammerland"). The dissolution was declared unconstitutional in 1979 because it violated the rights of the former State of Oldenburg, and the District was re-established in its old boundaries in 1980.

Friesland is also a [province](/wiki/Friesland "Friesland") in the northwest of the Netherlands.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Friesland_(district)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

In the east the district is bounded by the [Jade Bight](/wiki/Jade_Bight "Jade Bight"), a shallow bay of the North Sea. The island of [Wangerooge](/wiki/Wangerooge "Wangerooge"), one of the [East Frisian Islands](/wiki/East_Frisian_Islands "East Frisian Islands"), is a part of Friesland.

The inhabitants of Friesland insist that they are not a part of East Frisia. This is somewhat confusing, since Friesland is situated east of East Frisia, and geographers regard Friesland as a part of that region. The distinction has historical reasons: Friesland was an independent state from 1438 to 1575 (see above for details). Then and afterwards the region bore the name "Friesland", although this is also the German name for the entire [Frisia](/wiki/Frisia "Frisia").

Part of the District is in the [Lower Saxony Wadden Sea National Park](/wiki/Lower_Saxony_Wadden_Sea_National_Park "Lower Saxony Wadden Sea National Park").

## Coat of arms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Friesland_(district)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Coat of arms")]

The lion is from the arms of the chieftains of Jever; it was the symbol of the Lordship of Jever. The [blazon](/wiki/Blazon "Blazon") of the arms is: "Azure, a [lion](/wiki/Lion "Lion") rampant Or, armed and langued Gules, and in chief two [Greek crosses](/wiki/Greek_cross "Greek cross") Argent".
